description_long: This is a bulbous, terracotta-colored vase with a wide, flared rim and a narrow neck that expands into a rounded body. The vase features two small, rounded handles on opposite sides, positioned near the widest part of the body, and a subtle raised knob or decorative nub on one side. The surface appears matte and slightly rough, with faint vertical ridges and a hand-crafted, rustic finish. The rim is uneven and organic, suggesting artisanal craftsmanship rather than industrial production. The overall form is reminiscent of ancient Mediterranean or Middle Eastern pottery, suitable for decorative use in urban parks, plazas, or courtyards.
description: A rustic, terracotta-hued vase with a wide flared rim, rounded body, and two small side handles, featuring a matte, hand-crafted finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide flared rim, two small side handles, and a central raised knob on the body.
description_view_1: Rear view highlights the rounded back, absence of handles, and a visible protrusion at the base.
description_view_2: Side view displays both handles symmetrically and the smooth, uninterrupted curvature of the body.
description_view_3: Opposite rear view reveals the back protrusion and the organic, uneven rim shape.
